text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-datepicker repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: react-datepicker's date parser accepts a `strictParsing` prop. Previously, when `strictParsing` is false and a typed/pasted date string doesn't exactly match the configured `dateFormat`, the parser fell back to the JS engine's native `new Date(string)` constructor, which understands many common date/time formats regardless of `dateFormat`. That fallback is currently missing: with `strictParsing` false, `parseDate` (exported from `src/date_utils.ts`) now returns `null` for such flexible input instead of a valid `Date`. For example `parseDate("2025-12-16", "MM/dd/yyyy", undefined, false)` should resolve to December 16, 2025 (native `Date` can parse the ISO string even though it doesn't match `dateFormat`), but currently returns null. Two things must NOT regress while fixing this: when `strictParsing` is true there should be no native-parsing fallback (non-matching input still returns null), and when the input does match `dateFormat` exactly, that strict format-based result should still be preferred over the native fallback.

Environment notes: no root access is needed. Dependencies are already installed via `corepack enable && yarn install --immutable`. Run tests with Jest, e.g. `node_modules/.bin/jest src/test/date_utils_test.test.ts` to scope to the date-parsing tests. Fix the library source; do not just add workarounds in test files.

message: `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.
`--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.

text: I’ll inspect the current `parseDate` implementation and its focused tests, then patch the library behavior and run the date utility test suite plus any targeted checks for strict and format-matching cases.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "pwd && rg -n \"function parseDate|parseDate\\(\" src/date_utils.ts src/test/date_utils_test.test.ts && sed -n '1,240p' src/date_utils.ts && sed -n '1,260p' src/test/date_utils_test.test.ts"

M package.json
149d5470 fix: prevent crash when date props are passed as strings
78b9dfc9 fix: prevent webpack warning for optional date-fns-tz dependency
1ededb40 Add istanbul ignore comment for untestable catch block
19df127c Add fallback tests for timezone functions when date-fns-tz is not installed
2cd1b361 Add timezone support

text: The current function returns `null` immediately after all date-fns formats fail, so the missing behavior is localized. I’m checking repository history and date-fns/native validity semantics before editing, because the fallback must happen only after format parsing and must preserve the existing locale behavior.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc 'git show e4ed0316''^:src/date_utils.ts | sed -n '"'285,340p'; git show e4ed0316:src/date_utils.ts | sed -n '285,340p'; git show e4ed0316 -- src/date_utils.ts src/test/date_utils_test.test.ts"

text: The historical implementation confirms the regression: the removed branch used `new Date(value)` only in non-strict mode after date-fns parsing failed. I’ll restore that fallback after the configured formats have been attempted, so exact format matches still return first and strict mode remains format-only.

text: The focused parse behavior now works for the requested ISO case, and strict mode remains guarded. The existing suite contains one assertion that deliberately expects native-parsable input to stay `null` under a locale mismatch; that expectation conflicts with the restored contract, so I’m updating that test wording/expectation and adding explicit regression coverage for ISO fallback and strict-mode rejection.

text: Type checking and ESLint both pass. The only unrelated failure observed in the full date utility file is an existing `isTimeInDisabledRange` mock-based assertion; the complete `parseDate` subset passes with all 14 cases.

Implemented the fix in [src/date_utils.ts](/home/user/workspace/src/date_utils.ts:321):

- Non-strict parsing now falls back to `new Date(value)`.
- Strict parsing never uses native fallback.
- Format-based matches remain preferred.

Added regression tests in [date_utils_test.test.ts](/home/user/workspace/src/test/date_utils_test.test.ts:1097).
